operations.notes:
Fetches list of network objects from specific revision
The default value for get_total is false. For API calls that have pagination, set get_total to true on the first call. Use the value to returned in total to determine how many additional calls are required to retrieve all the desired results. To improve performance, omit or set get_total to false on any subsequent calls.
The show_members parameter is only relevant if type=group.
If type=group, the returned results will also include full details of every group member.
Set show_members=false to return only the name and ID of the members, and omit the full details of the group members.
Each UID in the 'uids' parameter can be given either with or without curly braces ('{}'). In case braces are used, they should be encoded. Example:
Instead of uids={58F8FA19-8A4F-7081-72D8-84ACAFD10D1B}, write uids=%7B58F8FA19-8A4F-7081-72D8-84ACAFD10D1B%7D ('{' encoded to '%7B' and '}' encoded to '%7D')

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Revision id

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Starting page for query, allowable type is numeric

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of pages for query starting from starting page, allowable type is numeric

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Network object name

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Network object type
parameters.allowableValues: basic, host, subnet, range, group, domain, any, installon, hostWithIfaces, user, user-group, alias, security_group, vm_instance, internet, reference, identity_awareness, domain-network

parameters.name: show_members
parameters.description: Set to false to return only the name and ID of the members for relevant types like "group"

parameters.name: contains_ip
parameters.description: Get only network objects containing this IP

parameters.name: identity_awareness
parameters.description: Get access role objects as "identity_awareness" if identity_awareness parameter is "supported". Default type returned is "identity_role"

parameters.name: typeOnDevice
parameters.description: Get only objects having this type_on_device

parameters.name: get_total
parameters.description: Set to true to get the total number of items that can be retrieved

parameters.name: add_parent_objects
parameters.description: Panorama only: Get objects from parent DGs (default true)

parameters.name: uids
parameters.description: Get only objects with the provided uids (single or comma separated)

responseMessages.message: The typeOnDevice value is not valid

operations.summary: Get specific network objects by revision
operations.notes:
Fetches a single or partial list of the network objects for a given revision.
For group network objects, the returned results will also include full details of every group member.
Set show_members=false to return only the name and ID of the members, and omit the full details of the group members.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: revision_id
parameters.description: Revision id

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Network object IDs separated by commas

parameters.name: show_members
parameters.description: Set to false to return only the name and ID of the members

parameters.name: identity_awareness
parameters.description: Get access role objects as "identity_awareness" if identity_awareness parameter is "supported". Default type returned is "identity_role"

operations.notes:
Fetches list of network objects defined on device given by ID
The default value for get_total is false. For API calls that have pagination, set get_total to true on the first call. Use the value to returned in total to determine how many additional calls are required to retrieve all the desired results. To improve performance, omit or set get_total to false on any subsequent calls.
The show_members parameter is only relevant if type=group.
If type=group, the returned results will also include full details of every group member.
Set show_members=false to return only the name and ID of the members, and omit the full details of the group members.
Each UID in the 'uids' parameter can be given either with or without curly braces ('{}'). In case braces are used, they should be encoded. Example:
Instead of uids={58F8FA19-8A4F-7081-72D8-84ACAFD10D1B}, write uids=%7B58F8FA19-8A4F-7081-72D8-84ACAFD10D1B%7D ('{' encoded to '%7B' and '}' encoded to '%7D')

parameters.description: Device ID

parameters.name: name

parameters.allowableValues: basic, host, subnet, range, group, domain, any, installon, hostWithIfaces, user, user-group, alias, security_group, vm_instance, internet, reference, identity_awareness, domain-network

parameters.description: Set to false to return only the name and ID of the members for relevant types like "group"

parameters.name: identity_awareness

operations.notes:
Fetches a single or partial list of the network objects for a given device ID.
For group network objects, the returned results will also include full details of every group member.
Set show_members=false to return only the name and ID of the members, and omit the full details of the group members.
Note: This API retrieves the information from the latest revision.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Device ID

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Network object IDs separated by commas

parameters.name: show_members
parameters.description: Set to false to return only the name and ID of the members

parameters.name: identity_awareness
parameters.description: Get access role objects as "identity_awareness" if identity_awareness parameter is "supported". Default type returned is "identity_role"

operations.summary: Get network objects matching specified criteria
operations.notes:
You can search for network objects where the IP address, name or comment fields have a specified text string,
or you can search for network objects that have an IP address that matches, contains or is contained in a specified IP subnet,
or you can search for network objects by uid.
examples:
https://192.168.1.1/securetrack/api/network_objects/search?filter=text&name=Access_Role&identity_awareness=supported ||{
	"network_objects": {
		"count": 1,
		"total": 1,
		"network_object": {
			"@xsi.type": "identityAwarenessDTO",
			"id": 169197,
			"name": "Access_Role_aaa",
			"display_name": "Access_Role_aaa",
			"class_name": "access_role",
			"type": "identity_awareness",
			"global": false,
			"device_id": 158,
			"comment": "",
			"uid": "{9EDC2607-6BEC-43D7-A99D-B522BA694ADA}",
			"implicit": false,
			"overrides": false,
			"type_on_device": "",
			"networks": {
				"network": {
					"@xsi.type": "basicNetworkObjectDTO",
					"id": 168419,
					"name": "Any",
					"display_name": "Any",
					"class_name": "any_object",
					"type": "basic",
					"global": false,
					"uid": "{97AEB369-9AEA-11D5-BD16-0090272CCB30}",
					"implicit": false,
					"overrides": false
				}
			},
			"users": {
				"user": {
					"id": 14317,
					"name": "ad_group_aaa",
					"display_name": "aaa",
					"class_name": "ad_group",
					"global": false,
					"comment": "",
					"uid": "{5D16859C-0256-4673-A402-4254588B8F40}",
					"dn": "CN=aaa,OU=SC_Users,DC=test,DC=com"
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Returns results for the specified domain ID.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Returns the results beginning with the specified result number.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Returns the specified number of results. If this field is not added or if a number greater than 100 is specified, the query uses "count=100".

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: "text" filter returns network objects where the IP address, name or comment fields have a specified text string. "subnet" filter returns network objects that have an IP address that matches, contains or is contained in a specified IP subnet. "uid" filter returns network objects with the specified uid. If no filter is specified, the "text" filter is used.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Returns network objects belonging to the specified device_id.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: (For "text" filter) You can specify "ip" to find network objects with the specified text in the IP address field.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: (For "text" filter) You can specify "name" to find network objects with the specified text in the name field.

parameters.name: comment
parameters.description: (For "text" filter) You can specify "comment" to find network objects with the specified text in the comment field.

parameters.name: any_field
parameters.description: (For "text" filter) You can specify "any_field" to find network objects with the specified text in the IP address, name or comment fields.

parameters.name: exact_match
parameters.description: (For "text" filter) You can add "exact_match=true" to find network objects with the specified text in the comment field. If this field is not specified, the query uses "exact_match=false".

parameters.name: contained_in
parameters.description: (For "subnet" filter) You can specify "contained_in" to find network objects that are contained in the specified IP subnet. For example, 10.1.0.0 is contained in 10.0.0.0.

parameters.name: contains
parameters.description: (For "subnet" filter) You can specify "contains" to find network objects that contain the specified IP subnet. For example, 10.0.0.0 contains 10.1.0.0.

parameters.name: exact_subnet
parameters.description: (For "subnet" filter) You can optionally specify "exact_subnet" to find network objects in a desired subnet mask. When not specified, a default mask is calculated according to the subnet class - e.g. 255.255.255.255 for a subnet of 1.1.1.1, 255.255.255.0 or a subnet of 1.1.1.0 etc.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: (for "uid" filter) Returns network objects with the specified uid.

parameters.name: identity_awareness
parameters.description: Get access role objects as "identity_awareness" if identity_awareness parameter is "supported". Default type returned is "identity_role"

parameters.name: typeOnDevice
parameters.description: You can specify  "typeOnDevice" to find network object groups that are EDL.

responseMessages.message: The typeOnDevice value is not valid.Use edl_ip_list as value to search EDL groups.

operations.summary: Get network groups containing specified network object
operations.notes: Returns groups that contain the specified network object

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Returns results for the specified domain ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The SecureTrack object ID of the network object.

operations.summary: Get rules containing specified network object
operations.notes: Returns the security rules that contain the specified network object

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Returns results for the specified domain ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The SecureTrack object ID of the network object.

parameters.name: include_groups
parameters.description: You can add "include_groups" to find rules that include the network object and rules that include the groups that include the network object.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Returns the results beginning with the specified result number.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Returns the specified number of results. If this field is not added or if a number greater than 100 is specified, the query uses "count=100".

negate.description: Negates the defined port(s), that is: the defined port(s) are ignored, and only all other logs are analyzed.

minSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the minimum source port number, ICMP Service: the ICMP type number.
